slides.title: Entrepreneurship and Business Success
texts: Mr. Bob Fox founded multiple successful companies starting in 1988, including Laser Life Inc., sold to a major corporation, Pitney Bowes, in 2007.​ Bob Fox established one of the first companies to create a recycling concept to address toner and plastic waste associated with laser printing technology.
for six consecutive years, emphasizing customer loyalty and satisfaction.​
Pioneered early recycling methods to reduce toner and plastic waste in laser printing technology.​
Competitive drive, positive work environment, and customer happiness define his respected leadership approach.​
slides.manualVisualSummary: Historic 'first office' photo labelled 1988 with a family-owned-and-operated badge.

Be the advisor, not the order-taker
Lead

Create clarity and momentum for every prospect

diagramTexts: Connect
Connect — warm tone, real interest
Label
Label — identify their gap or issue
Ask
Ask — pull, don’t push
Bridge
Bridge — show the path forward
Close
Close — make it easy to say yes

slides.title: Major Customer Objections
diagramTexts: Signed new agreement — ask term & distance in
Don’t know lease end — COS offers more than copiers
Not interested — ask reason
Happy with vendor — loyalty valued, backup helps
Corporate decides — identify point of contact
We own equipment — ask age & who services it
Price — discuss cost vs. price
Toner concerns — COS makes compatible, not recycled

slides.title: Killer Questions (Consultative)
diagramTexts: How are you currently managing your print environment?
Know your annual print/imaging spend?
Are you using eco‑friendly solutions?
Know eco‑friendly pounds of plastic thrown away?
What’s your plan when the lease ends?
Know your cost per page? Aware it increases yearly?
How much time goes to managing supplies?
Walk‑time/downtime analysis done?
Describe your workflow?
What has your current vendor done to impress you? WOW

slides.title: Identifying a Machine
slides.manualVisualSummary: Sharp printer naming convention visual that labels the BP model prefix, series, version, color or mono code, and print speed.

slides.title: Printer / MFP / Copier
diagramTexts: Printer
– Great for teams that only need simple output. Fast, efficient, and compact… but strictly print-only. No scanning, copying, or workflows.
MFP (Multi-Function Printer)
– Your all-in-one work hub. Print, scan, copy, fax, automate workflows, send to email/drive, and streamline the entire office from one device.
Copier
– Built for heavier environments. Higher duty cycles, finishing options, larger paper capacity, and stronger long-term cost savings for teams with consistent volume.
slides.manualVisualSummary: Product comparison visual pairing example printer, multifunction printer, and floor copier images.
Acquisition options graphic comparing lease (60, 48, or 36 month terms), rental (12-month option), and purchase (upfront payment).

diagramTexts: Base fee includes remote monitoring software & more!
Free B/W pages for onboarding
Color: $0.07
BP Series:
Cheaper rates due to improved toner tech

slides.title: CPP Option #2 Inclusive Plan
diagramTexts: Includes average monthly volume or a specific page allowance.. It mainly means “all you can print for one price”
Pages do NOT roll to next month
Ideal for Predictable Monthly Bills

slides.title: Toner Basics
diagramTexts: Most HP printers use low-yield (a) and high-yield (x) cartridges
Educate clients to avoid mis-orders and downtime
High Yeild hold significantly more toner. Often 2 to 3x the page yield
High yield reduces having to replace the cartridge as ofter and reduces downtime from running out of toner

slides.title: What is MICR Toner?
diagramTexts: MICR toner is for printing checks
Formula creates signal strength for bank check readers
Enables routing number + account number recognition

slides.title: OEM vs Compatible Toner
diagramTexts: OEM = Original Equipment Manufacturer (HP, Xerox, Lexmark)
OEM toner comes in manufacturer packaging, sold in big-box stores COS compatible toner offers same performance at lower cost
Our COS toner is manufactured in-house for higher margins & control

slides.title: Why Choose COS Toner Cartridges
diagramTexts: Manufactured in the USA
Success rate higher than 99%
Extra toner added for higher yield
Rigorously tested before distribution
Higher density, vibrant prints
Free recycling options for up to 95% of plastic
Lower printing costs by up to 20%
Lifetime warranty
Get more from your supplies

slides.title: Auto-Supply & Auto-Restock Program
diagramTexts: Keep supply levels stocked with zero effort
We ship toner as soon as your printer runs low
Optional monthly shipment schedule
Removes supply closet chaos & downtime
Free service: COS manages your supply closet for you
slides.manualVisualSummary: Circular auto-restock icon that reinforces supply automation.

slides.title: Cartridge Collection Program
diagramTexts: Free and convenient recycling service
We provide toner collection boxes
Fill with empties, COS picks them up periodically
Supports sustainability & reduces landfill waste
Foot in the door for sales as we can actively review the usage report!!

slides.title: Client Pre‑Approval Requirements
diagramTexts: Business must show 2 years active registration via Secretary of State
Use: https://ecorp.sos.ga.gov/BusinessSearch
Delivery & billing address required
Model selected, lease term, and monthly rate needed

slides.title: If Secretary of State Shows Less Than 2 Years
texts: If Secretary of State Shows Less Than 2 Years
diagramTexts: Client can sign a Personal Guarantee (PG)
Client can provide another active company they own
We can file as DBA under the active company
We can offer a rental option instead of lease
This flexibility separates COS from other dealers

slides.title: What You Need to Place a Sales Order
texts: What You Need to Place a Sales Order
diagramTexts: Onsite Point of Contact name, number, and email
Any specific installation instructions
Full Bill-To & Ship-To info (from Sales Order Form)
Model selected + quantity + accessories
Lease term, leasing company, FMV or $1 buyout
Monthly base rate and CPP details
Pickup equipment info if applicable

slides.title: Sales Order Form Required Details
diagramTexts: Customer Bill-To: Name, address, AP email
Customer Ship-To (if different): Name & address
Meter contact’s email
Item number, model/description, serial numbers (if used)
Unit cost & unit price fields
Pickup equipment (make/model/serial)
Lease payment + rate + term
Service Agreement selection (Yes/No, Buyout Included?)
Covered pages + CPP rates

slides.title: Approval → Lease → Delivery Flowchart
diagramTexts: 1. Pre‑Approval: SOS check, addresses, model, term, rate
2. Credit Options: PG, DBA, rental if under 2 yrs active
3. Proposal Presented: equipment + service plan
4. Client Accepts: confirm install instructions & POC
5. Lease Docs Issued: FMV or $1 buyout + term selected
6. Lease Signed & Submitted: funding pending install
7. Equipment Ordered & Scheduled
8. Delivery & Installation Completed
9. Funding Released & Service Begins
slides.manualVisualSummary: Handshake photo used behind the approval-to-delivery flowchart.

slides.title: Why COS Approvals Are Easier!
diagramTexts: Flexible approvals for new businesses (<2 yrs)
Personal Guarantee option
